title: Phase 1: Foundation
description: initech init + initech up work end-to-end. A user can bootstrap a project and start a tmux session with agents from a single config file.
acceptance_criteria: Run initech init in a fresh directory with a valid initech.yaml, then initech up. A tmux session starts with super and eng1 windows, each running Claude with correct permissions and CLAUDE.md files. Manual verification by Nelson.

title: Implement internal/exec package
description: Create the exec.Runner interface and DefaultRunner implementation. This is the leaf dependency that every other package uses to shell out to external commands (tmux, git, bd, tmuxinator). Wraps os/exec with consistent error handling. Must support Run(name, args) and RunInDir(dir, name, args).
design: Single file exec.go with interface + default impl. Test file exec_test.go verifies real command execution (echo, ls) and error cases (nonexistent binary). No external dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Runner interface defined with Run() and RunInDir() methods. DefaultRunner implementation shells out via os/exec. All tests pass. Package doc comment explains purpose and concurrency safety.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Implemented exec.Runner interface with DefaultRunner (os/exec) and FakeRunner (test helper). 7 tests: Run, RunInDir, trim, empty dir, nonexistent binary, non-zero exit, FakeRunner recording. go vet clean.

title: Implement cmd/up command
description: Wire up the initech up command. Thin wrapper around tmuxinator start. Loads config, verifies tmuxinator config exists, runs tmuxinator start <project>. Reports session name and agent count on success.
design: cmd/up.go with cobra command. Load config, check for tmuxinator YAML, exec tmuxinator start via exec.Runner. Count roles from config for the success message.
acceptance_criteria: initech up loads initech.yaml, verifies ~/.config/tmuxinator/<project>.yml exists, runs tmuxinator start. Prints 'Session <name> started with N agents.' on success. Error if no initech.yaml found. Error if tmuxinator config missing (suggest running initech init).

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. cmd/up loads config via Discover, verifies tmuxinator YAML exists, runs tmuxinator start, prints agent count. Error messages guide user to initech init.

title: Implement internal/config package
description: Create the config types (Project, Repo, BeadsConfig, RoleOverride), Load() to read initech.yaml, and Validate() to enforce constraints. This package owns the initech.yaml schema. Does not know about files on disk, tmux, or git beyond reading its own config file.
design: config.go with types + Load + Validate. Config discovery: --config flag, ./initech.yaml, walk upward. Use yaml.v3 for unmarshaling. Test with table-driven tests covering valid configs, missing fields, invalid references.
acceptance_criteria: Go types match systemdesign section 2.2. Load() reads YAML from path, returns Project. Validate() checks: project required, root required and expandable, roles non-empty, grid subset of roles, role_overrides keys in roles. All tests pass including validation edge cases.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Config types, Load, Discover (walk-upward), Validate, Write, expandHome. 14 tests covering valid config, missing fields, duplicate roles, grid/override validation, roundtrip write, home expansion.

title: Implement internal/roles/catalog package
description: Create the role catalog: RoleDef type, PermissionTier enum, the Catalog map of all 11 well-known roles, and LookupRole() function. Open catalog design: unknown role names return a sensible default (Autonomous, no src, no playbooks).
design: catalog.go with types and map. Single LookupRole function. No dependencies on other internal packages. Test verifies each catalog entry has correct permission tier and flags.
acceptance_criteria: RoleDef struct with Name, Permission, NeedsSrc, NeedsPlaybooks, NeedsMakefile, Template fields. All 11 roles defined per systemdesign section 3.1. LookupRole returns catalog entry for known roles, default for unknown. Tests cover all catalog entries and unknown role fallback.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. 13 roles in catalog with correct permission tiers and flags. LookupRole with open catalog (unknown roles get Autonomous defaults). 8 tests covering completeness, tiers, src/playbooks/makefile flags, name consistency, known/unknown lookup.

title: Implement internal/roles/render package
description: Create the template rendering system: RenderVars struct, Render() function with regex-based {{variable}} substitution. Matches the beads pattern. Unknown variables left as-is. No conditionals or loops.
design: render.go with compiled regex pattern, RenderVars struct, Render function. Pattern: regexp.MustCompile(\{\{(\w+)\}\}). Map-based lookup from struct fields. No text/template dependency.
acceptance_criteria: RenderVars struct with ProjectName, ProjectRoot, RepoURL, TechStack, BuildCmd, TestCmd, BeadsPrefix. Render() substitutes all known variables, leaves unknown ones intact. No runtime errors on missing vars. Tests cover: full substitution, partial vars, unknown vars preserved, empty string vars.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Render() with regex {{variable}} substitution and RenderString() convenience. 7 tests: full, partial, unknown preserved, no vars, all vars, multiple occurrences, RenderString.

title: Implement internal/roles/templates (super + eng only)
description: Create the first two role templates as inline Go string constants: SuperTemplate and EngTemplate. Also create the four document templates (PRDTemplate, SpecTemplate, SystemDesignTemplate, RoadmapTemplate). These prove the template pattern works before building all 11 role templates in Phase 4.
design: templates.go with const SuperTemplate, const EngTemplate. doctemplates.go with const PRDTemplate, SpecTemplate, SystemDesignTemplate, RoadmapTemplate. Content follows spec section 2 role definitions and systemdesign section 3.4/3.5. Test verifies each template renders without error and contains expected section headers.
acceptance_criteria: SuperTemplate covers: identity, critical failure modes, decision authority, responsibilities, constraints, artifacts, workflow, communication, cross-role boundaries. EngTemplate covers the same structure for engineers. Four document templates match systemdesign section 3.5. All templates use {{variable}} placeholders that render.Render() can substitute. Templates are 100-200 lines each for roles, 30-60 lines each for documents.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. SuperTemplate (~90 lines), EngTemplate (~90 lines), QATemplate (~55 lines). Four doc templates: PRD, Spec, SystemDesign, Roadmap. 7 test cases: render verification, section presence, role_name preservation, Phase 0 content in roadmap.

title: Implement internal/tmuxinator package
description: Generate tmuxinator YAML from an initech config. Produces both main session YAML and grid session YAML as []byte. Does not write files (scaffold does that). Does not know about scaffold, git, or tmux runtime.
design: tmuxinator.go with Generate and GenerateGrid functions. Build YAML structure in Go structs, marshal with yaml.v3. Window startup command: supervised roles get 'claude --continue', autonomous get 'claude --continue --dangerously-skip-permissions'. Depends on config types and roles/catalog for permission tier lookup.
acceptance_criteria: Generate() takes a config.Project and returns main session YAML. GenerateGrid() returns grid session YAML. Output matches the tmuxinator pattern from spec section 1.2: session name, project root, pre_window with BEADS_DIR export, windows with role names, correct startup commands per permission tier. Grid YAML uses layout tiled. Tests verify YAML structure, correct window count, permission-appropriate commands.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Generate() and GenerateGrid() producing tmuxinator YAML. Correct permission-tier commands, pre_window BEADS_DIR, grid naming. 8 tests: structure, window count, permissions, pre_window, empty grid, unknown roles.

title: Implement internal/scaffold package
description: Create the project tree on disk from a config. Creates directories, writes rendered CLAUDE.md files per role, writes root CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .gitignore, docs/ with four document templates. Idempotent: checks os.Stat() before writing, existing files not overwritten unless force=true.
design: scaffold.go with Scaffold(project, runner, force) function. Uses roles/catalog to determine what each role needs (src dir, playbooks dir, makefile). Uses roles/render to substitute template variables. Writes files with os.WriteFile. Tests use t.TempDir() for isolation.
acceptance_criteria: Scaffold() takes config.Project and creates the full directory tree per spec section 4.2. Role directories created with CLAUDE.md from rendered templates. docs/ created with prd.md, spec.md, systemdesign.md, roadmap.md from document templates. Root CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md written. .gitignore written. Existing files preserved unless force flag set. Tests verify directory structure, file contents, idempotency.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Scaffold creates full project tree: root dir, .gitignore, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, docs/ with 4 document templates, role dirs with CLAUDE.md (template-selected per role), .claude/, src/, playbooks/. Idempotent with force override. RoleOverrides applied. 10 tests.

title: Implement internal/git package
description: Shell out to git for repo initialization, submodule management, and commits. Uses exec.Runner for all command execution. Does not know about config or tmux.
design: git.go with Init, AddSubmodule, Commit functions. Each takes an exec.Runner and relevant parameters. Uses RunInDir for directory-scoped operations. Error wrapping with context.
acceptance_criteria: Init() runs git init in a directory (skip if .git exists). AddSubmodule() runs git submodule add. Commit() stages and commits with a message. All functions take exec.Runner as parameter. Tests use a fake Runner to verify correct git commands are invoked without requiring real git.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. Init (skip existing), AddSubmodule, CommitAll. All use exec.Runner. 7 tests with FakeRunner verifying correct git commands, directory scoping, error wrapping.

title: Implement cmd/init command
description: Wire up the initech init command. Orchestrates the full bootstrap flow: interactive setup (or load existing initech.yaml), validate config, scaffold project tree, init git, add submodules, init beads, generate tmuxinator configs, initial commit. This is the composition root that connects all internal packages.
design: cmd/init.go with cobra command. Reads or creates config, calls scaffold.Scaffold, git.Init, git.AddSubmodule for each NeedsSrc role, shells out to bd init, writes tmuxinator YAML to ~/.config/tmuxinator/, runs git add + commit. Uses exec.DefaultRunner for real execution.
acceptance_criteria: initech init in an empty directory with interactive prompts creates: initech.yaml, .beads/, .gitignore, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, docs/ with four documents, role directories with CLAUDE.md files, tmuxinator config at ~/.config/tmuxinator/. initech init with existing initech.yaml skips interactive setup. Graceful degradation: skip beads if bd not found, still generate tmuxinator YAML if tmuxinator not installed. Print summary of created files.

comments.author: Nelson Melo
comments.text: DONE. cmd/init orchestrates full bootstrap: load or interactive setup, validate config, scaffold project tree, git init, submodule add, beads init (graceful degradation), tmuxinator YAML generation, initial commit, summary output. Smoke tested end-to-end: initech init with config file creates all expected directories, files, docs, role CLAUDE.md files, tmuxinator config.
